summary:
Standardization methods can vary depending on context; thus using only one nation’s population does not represent flexibility required by these techniques. Different studies emphasize adaptability over uniformity when applying standardized approaches due to geographical and demographic differences needing tailored adjustments instead of relying solely on nationally defined standards.
To be continued...
In conclusion, while other options may hold validity concerning principles underlying successful application statistical tools within public health research settings including but limited scope applicability beyond conventional boundaries set forth traditionally restrictive interpretations emphasizing local relevance remains paramount ensuring accurate representation varied subpopulations
